fields.description:
## What
Read-only derivations over the existing gate sidecars for gate-economics + env-diagnosis metrics: an LLM cost proxy (`llm_calls`, latency) from the plan/code-review sidecars, cost-per-accepted-change, first-pass verification, and env-diagnosis intervals (`gate_error_v1` ERROR → next PASS). All read-only; nothing is written.

## Data-access ground truth (the plan is built on this)
- **Only `REVIEW_RESULT` (`plan_review_result_v2`) carries a `metrics` block** — `{det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, llm_calls, claim_path}` (`plan_review/sidecar.py:496-497`). **`COMPLETION_VERDICT` carries NO metrics block** (verified in `completion_sidecar.py:182-219`; neither the pass nor fail branch has `llm_calls`). So all `llm_calls`/latency derivations read `REVIEW_RESULT.metrics` ONLY — the completion sidecar is used for the pass/fail *outcome*, never for cost.
- **"Accepted change"** is defined precisely: a `completion_verifier_pass_v1` record (`SCHEMA_PASS`, `completion_sidecar.py:29`) on a ticket — i.e. the completion (close) gate PASSED. This is NOT a plan-review PASS (`plan_review_result_v2` with `verdict=="PASS"`), which only clears the *claim* gate and does not mean the ticket closed. The reader distinguishes the two by schema.
- Sidecars are reducer-ignored; read them via the raw event files or the ready helpers `rebar.llm.plan_review.sidecar.all_review_results(id, repo_root=)` and `rebar.llm.completion_sidecar.latest_pass_record(id, repo_root=)`. `gate_error_v1` (ERROR) rides the host stream (`REVIEW_RESULT`/`COMPLETION_VERDICT`) and is invisible to the schema-guarded verdict readers, so ERROR/PASS pairing uses a raw ordered scan of those event files (timestamp = the event envelope `timestamp` / filename ns-prefix).

## Files
- `src/rebar/metrics/sidecar_metrics.py` — new: reader over the sidecars, registered into `src/rebar/metrics/registry.py`.
- `tests/unit/metrics/test_sidecar_metrics.py` — new.

## Metric definitions
- **llm_call_proxy / latency** — sum `REVIEW_RESULT.metrics.llm_calls` (and latency fields) over the range. `source="sidecar"`.
- **cost_per_accepted_change** — summed `REVIEW_RESULT.metrics.llm_calls` divided by the count of `completion_verifier_pass_v1` records (accepted closes) in range; returns `None` (→ `Unavailable`) when there are zero accepted closes (no division by zero).
- **first_pass_verification** — fraction of tickets whose FIRST `COMPLETION_VERDICT` record is a `completion_verifier_pass_v1` (passed on the first verification attempt).
- **env_diagnosis_interval** — pair each `gate_error_v1` ERROR record with the next PASS record on the SAME ticket's stream (raw ordered scan); the interval = PASS `timestamp` − ERROR `timestamp`. An ERROR with no following PASS is an open (unclosed) diagnosis interval.

## Approach
Pure read-side derivations over sidecars that already persist per-run metrics, verdicts, and (via ticket 8bc5) the additive ERROR outcome. `confidence="high"`; `is_authoritative("sidecar")` is True. Nothing is written.

## Tests
- Unit (real sidecar fixtures written to a temp store): cost-per-accepted-change over 3 REVIEW_RESULT runs (llm_calls 2+3+4=9) with 1 `completion_verifier_pass_v1` returns 9; with 0 accepted returns `None`. An ERROR record paired with a later PASS on the same ticket yields an interval equal to the fixture timestamp delta; an ERROR with no PASS yields an open interval.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/unit/metrics/test_sidecar_metrics.py` passes: a `gate_error_v1` ERROR row paired with a later PASS on the same ticket yields a diagnosis-interval duration equal to the fixture timestamp delta (`assert dur == expected`).
- [ ] cost_per_accepted_change over a fixture of 3 REVIEW_RESULT runs summing `llm_calls==9` with exactly 1 `completion_verifier_pass_v1` returns 9; a fixture with 0 accepted closes returns `None` (→ `Unavailable`, no ZeroDivisionError) — both asserted.
- [ ] the cost derivations read `llm_calls` ONLY from `REVIEW_RESULT.metrics` (never from COMPLETION_VERDICT, which has none) — asserted by a fixture whose COMPLETION_VERDICT records carry no metrics yet cost is still computed from the REVIEW_RESULT runs.
- [ ] the metrics register into `src/rebar/metrics/registry.py` with `source="sidecar"` (authoritative) — asserted.
